title: hashfm-mutter Block
description: Schema for script hashfm blocks used by mutter. Extends hashfm-agent with mutter-specific fields (arguments, type). Supports single-command and multi-command forms. name and path are always derived from the filesystem and are never declared in the block.

type-category.description: A broad data category used for chain compatibility validation.

type.description: Declares the data categories this command consumes and produces. Used exclusively for chain validation — format specifics are the script's concern. If present, at least one of input or output must be set.

input.description: Data categories this command accepts as input. A chain is valid when this intersects with the upstream command's output.

output.description: Data categories this command produces as output. A chain is valid when this intersects with the downstream command's input.

argument.description: Describes a single named argument extracted from user input.

pattern.description: A regular expression used to extract this argument's value from the user's natural language prompt.

description.description: A short plain-language description of what this argument represents.

command.description: A single command entry — either the whole script or one subcommand within a multi-command script.

description.description: A single line describing what this command does. Written in the imperative. No trailing punctuation.

usage.description: A single line showing how to invoke this command. Placeholders are written as {{argument_name}}.

arguments.description: A map of argument names to argument descriptors. Each key must match a {{placeholder}} in the usage field.

exits.description: A map of exit codes to plain-language descriptions.

single-command.description: A block for a script with a single invocation form.

multi-command.description: A block for a script that exposes multiple subcommands. A top-level sequence — no wrapper key.
